prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
Verily, I, Brother Aldric of the Scriveners’ Guild, didst witness the most mystifying quarterly reckoning: the Lordly SaaS folk spake not of bushels or coin, but of "recurring revenue" and "net churn," as though gold flowed from enchanted clouds rather than honest mines or tilled soil.

Yea, the Exchequer showed great mirth at gains unseen—for no sacks of silver graced their vaults, only flickering glyphs upon glass tablets that vanisheth if the hearth-fire (which they call "servers") doth grow cold.

Perplexed am I by their "burn rate," for unlike proper hearths that consume honest wood, theirs devoureth phantom florins to conjure naught but vaporous tools for distant yeomen to squabble o’er spreadsheets as thick as abbey ledgers.

And lo! Their "growth metrics" soared like phoenixes, yet no new serfs toiled in their fields—only spectral "users" multiplied in the aether, paying not in barley nor oxen, but in promises sealed with digital sigils that flicker like will-o’-the-wisps.
